| 3 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 11 | `charlie@example.com` |

**Summary:** All three are found in `UsersController.scala` (lines 9–11), where they are embedded directly in hardcoded `User(...)` object literals — likely used as seed/fixture data. These emails belong to placeholder users *Alice*, *Bob*, and *Charlie*.

**Recommendation:** Hardcoded user data (including emails) should be moved out of source code and into a database seed file, environment-specific config, or a test fixtures file — to avoid committing potentially real email addresses to version control and to keep business logic cleanly separated from data.
